data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider GE Vernova (GEV) After A 168.5% One-Year Surge?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether GE Vernova's current share price really matches its potential, you are not alone. Many investors are trying to work out what a fair value looks like for this stock.
  • The share price recently closed at US$817.35, with returns of 20.3% year to date and 168.5% over the last year, alongside 7.4% and 6.4% declines across the past 7 and 30 days respectively.
  • Recent moves have come as investors continue to react to GE Vernova's position within the broader capital goods space and its exposure to power grid and energy transition themes. These factors often shape how investors think about both the potential and the risks attached to the current price level.
  • Even so, GE Vernova currently records a valuation score of 1 out of 6. This raises questions about how different methods such as DCFs, multiples and market based checks compare, and sets up a closer look at those tools along with an even more rounded way to think about value later in the article.

GE Vernova scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: GE Vernova Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model estimates what a company might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today, using a required rate of return. It focuses on the cash the business could generate for shareholders rather than reported earnings.

For GE Vernova, the latest twelve month free cash flow is about $3.95b. Analysts provide detailed projections out to 2030, with free cash flow for that year estimated at $10.86b. Beyond the explicit analyst period, Simply Wall St extends the forecast using its own assumptions, giving a ten year path of cash flows that are then discounted back to present value.

Using this 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, the estimated intrinsic value comes out at about $716.75 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $817.35, the model implies the stock is about 14.0% overvalued on this set of cash flow assumptions.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests GE Vernova may be overvalued by 14.0%. Discover 62 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for GE Vernova.

Approach 2: GE Vernova Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about value because it connects what you pay per share to the earnings that support that price. Higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk often justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk typically point to a lower, more cautious multiple.

GE Vernova currently trades on a P/E of 45.11x. The average P/E for the Electrical industry is about 30.65x, and the peer group used here sits at 42.61x, so the stock is priced above both of these reference points. Simply Wall St also provides a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 73.56x. This figure reflects what P/E might be expected after factoring in elements such as earnings growth profile, profit margins, industry, market cap and company specific risks.

Because the Fair Ratio incorporates these fundamentals, it can offer a more tailored yardstick than a simple comparison with sector or peer averages. Setting GE Vernova’s current P/E of 45.11x against the Fair Ratio of 73.56x suggests the shares are trading below that model based reference level, which indicates the stock may appear undervalued on this measure.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 20 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your GE Vernova Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives on Simply Wall St's Community page give you a simple way to combine your view of GE Vernova's story with your own forecasts for revenue, earnings and margins, link that to a Fair Value, and then keep it up to date as news or earnings arrive. This is why one investor might build a high growth Narrative that points to a Fair Value around US$1,019 per share, while another uses more cautious assumptions and lands closer to US$538.80, giving each of them a clear, numbers based reference for whether the current price sits above or below their own estimate of worth.

For GE Vernova however we will make it really easy for you with previews of two leading GE Vernova Narratives:

🐂 GE Vernova Bull Case

Fair value: US$861.62 per share

Implied pricing: about 5.1% below that fair value based on the recent close of US$817.35

Analyst modeled revenue growth: 15.0% a year

  • Analysts see demand for electrification and decarbonization, a growing installed base and higher margin services as key supports for revenue and earnings over time.
  • They build in rising profit margins and share count reduction, using a discount rate of 9.29% and a future P/E of 33.8x to arrive at the US$861.62 fair value and consensus target.
  • The narrative still flags risks in European demand, wind losses, project and tariff exposure and restructuring execution, so the outcome depends on how those pressures evolve.

🐻 GE Vernova Bear Case

Fair value: US$538.80 per share

Implied pricing: about 51.7% above that fair value based on the recent close of US$817.35

Analyst modeled revenue growth: 11.3% a year

  • This more cautious view focuses on geopolitical and policy risk, competition and cost pressures as potential constraints on revenue growth and margins.
  • Bearish analysts model lower growth, a different margin path and a higher discount rate than the consensus case, which together point to a US$538.80 fair value.
  • Even in this scenario the narrative acknowledges a sizeable backlog, ongoing productivity efforts and capital returns, but treats current market expectations as demanding.

data.title: Compass Group Insiders Sell US$4.3m Of Stock, Possibly Signalling Caution
data.description:
Many Compass Group PLC (LON:CPG) insiders ditched their stock over the past year, which may be of interest to the company's shareholders. Knowing whether insiders are buying is usually more helpful when evaluating insider transactions, as insider selling can have various explanations. However, when multiple insiders sell stock over a specific duration, shareholders should take notice as that could possibly be a red flag.

While we would never suggest that investors should base their decisions solely on what the directors of a company have been doing, we do think it is perfectly logical to keep tabs on what insiders are doing.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

The Last 12 Months Of Insider Transactions At Compass Group

The Group CEO & Director, Dominic Blakemore, made the biggest insider sale in the last 12 months. That single transaction was for UK£2.5m worth of shares at a price of UK£23.41 each. While insider selling is a negative, to us, it is more negative if the shares are sold at a lower price. The silver lining is that this sell-down took place above the latest price (UK£20.80). So it is hard to draw any strong conclusion from it.

Happily, we note that in the last year insiders paid UK£363k for 15.24k shares. But they sold 183.51k shares for UK£4.3m. All up, insiders sold more shares in Compass Group than they bought, over the last year. The chart below shows insider transactions (by companies and individuals) over the last year. By clicking on the graph below, you can see the precise details of each insider transaction!

See our latest analysis for Compass Group

If you are like me, then you will not want to miss this free list of small cap stocks that are not only being bought by insiders but also have attractive valuations.

Insiders At Compass Group Have Bought Stock Recently

Over the last quarter, Compass Group insiders have spent a meaningful amount on shares. Group CFO & Director Petros Parras spent UK£353k on stock, and there wasn't any selling. This makes one think the business has some good points.

Insider Ownership

For a common shareholder, it is worth checking how many shares are held by company insiders. Usually, the higher the insider ownership, the more likely it is that insiders will be incentivised to build the company for the long term. Compass Group insiders own about UK£22m worth of shares. That equates to 0.06% of the company. We've certainly seen higher levels of insider ownership elsewhere, but these holdings are enough to suggest alignment between insiders and the other shareholders.

So What Do The Compass Group Insider Transactions Indicate?

The recent insider purchase is heartening. However, the longer term transactions are not so encouraging. We don't take much heart from transactions by Compass Group insiders over the last year. But they own a reasonable amount of the company, and there was some buying recently. In short they are likely aligned with shareholders. In addition to knowing about insider transactions going on, it's beneficial to identify the risks facing Compass Group. Every company has risks, and we've spotted 1 warning sign for Compass Group you should know about.

But note: Compass Group may not be the best stock to buy. So take a peek at this free list of interesting companies with high ROE and low debt.

For the purposes of this article, insiders are those individuals who report their transactions to the relevant regulatory body. We currently account for open market transactions and private dispositions of direct interests only, but not derivative transactions or indirect interests.

data.title: SOL S.p.A. (BIT:SOL) Yearly Results: Here's What Analysts Are Forecasting For This Year
data.description:
Shareholders of SOL S.p.A. (BIT:SOL) will be pleased this week, given that the stock price is up 14% to €58.50 following its latest annual results. Results were roughly in line with estimates, with revenues of €1.8b and statutory earnings per share of €1.84. The analysts typically update their forecasts at each earnings report, and we can judge from their estimates whether their view of the company has changed or if there are any new concerns to be aware of. So we collected the latest post-earnings statutory consensus estimates to see what could be in store for next year.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Following the latest results, SOL's four analysts are now forecasting revenues of €1.91b in 2026. This would be a satisfactory 5.7% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to rise 8.8% to €2.00. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of €1.90b and earnings per share (EPS) of €2.00 in 2026. So it's pretty clear that, although the analysts have updated their estimates, there's been no major change in expectations for the business following the latest results.

Check out our latest analysis for SOL

There were no changes to revenue or earnings estimates or the price target of €56.63, suggesting that the company has met expectations in its recent result. The consensus price target is just an average of individual analyst targets, so - it could be handy to see how wide the range of underlying estimates is. There are some variant perceptions on SOL, with the most bullish analyst valuing it at €59.00 and the most bearish at €55.00 per share. This is a very narrow spread of estimates, implying either that SOL is an easy company to value, or - more likely - the analysts are relying heavily on some key assumptions.

Of course, another way to look at these forecasts is to place them into context against the industry itself. It's pretty clear that there is an expectation that SOL's revenue growth will slow down substantially, with revenues to the end of 2026 expected to display 5.7% growth on an annualised basis. This is compared to a historical growth rate of 12% over the past five years. Compare this to the 6 other companies in this industry with analyst coverage, which are forecast to grow their revenue at 6.5% per year. So it's pretty clear that, while SOL's revenue growth is expected to slow, it's expected to grow roughly in line with the industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most obvious conclusion is that there's been no major change in the business' prospects in recent times, with the analysts holding their earnings forecasts steady, in line with previous estimates. Happily, there were no real changes to revenue forecasts, with the business still expected to grow in line with the overall industry. There was no real change to the consensus price target, suggesting that the intrinsic value of the business has not undergone any major changes with the latest estimates.

Keeping that in mind, we still think that the longer term trajectory of the business is much more important for investors to consider. We have forecasts for SOL going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

It might also be worth considering whether SOL's debt load is appropriate, using our debt analysis tools on the Simply Wall St platform, here.

In the last twelve months, the biggest single purchase by an insider was when CEO & Executive Director Neil Crabb bought UK£100k worth of shares at a price of UK£0.16 per share. Their view may have changed since then, but at least it shows they felt optimistic at the time. To us, it's very important to consider the price insiders pay for shares. It is generally more encouraging if they paid above the current price, as it suggests they saw value, even at higher levels.
